comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "Plan-review gate in self-referential infinite regress after 5 remediation rounds (blocking 8->2->1->1->1; each residual block is a 'no-AC-for-the-detail-just-added' completeness loop, not a safety gap). All SUBSTANTIVE safety findings addressed: ADF-rendered marker detection, reuse of outbound_comments._normalize_comment_body for the dedup key, paginated get_comments + maxResults=0 total reconciliation, single 429 route via dispatch_one._call_with_retry, keep-oldest dedup, backup+completeness gate, dry-run default, rollback, reconciler quiescing, deletion log. Proceeding to produce the READ-ONLY backup + dry-run per the user's explicit session directive (backup -> dry-run -> human approval -> execute). The IRREVERSIBLE deletion stays gated on explicit human approval of the dry-run.".

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: DRY-RUN COMPLETE (read-only, no deletions) — all 13 tickets, artifacts in $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp/jira-cleanup/. Each ticket: 5000/5000 comments (all at cap; fetch==reported total, completeness OK). ALL 65,000 comments are bot-marked (RECONCILER_MARKER in adf_to_text(body)); 0 human comments on any ticket. Single bot accountId across all 13: 712020:6471376f-4e5e-4ed2-8c05-330827bc387e (homogeneous cross-check PASS). Keep oldest of each distinct _normalize_comment_body -> distinct bodies kept per ticket: REB-155=69(rec76), 352=64, 428=31, 341=30, 289=24(rec29), 427=27, 183=25, 327=25, 424=25, 425=25, 190=22, 426=22, 453=22. A few distinct counts are BELOW bd80's recorded local-distinct (155:69v76, 289:24v29) because those local comments normalize-collide on the Jira side — NO Jira content lost (every distinct normalized body kept once), and it matches the reconciler's own dedup. TOTAL to delete: 64,589 (411 kept). Execute is IRREVERSIBLE + throttled ~multi-hour. AWAITING explicit human approval before --execute.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: EXECUTE IN PROGRESS (human-approved). Backup: all 13 tickets fully backed up (222MB, id/body/author/created, verified 5000/5000 each) at $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp/jira-cleanup/backup/ BEFORE any deletion. Deleting via 5-way concurrent DELETE /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/comment/{id} with 429/Retry-After backoff; keep-oldest-of-each-distinct-normalized-body; log at jira-cleanup/deletions-run.jsonl (per-line ticket/id/body_hash/status). Resumable via per-ticket done-markers. Checkpoint: ~10k deletes done (204 success + idempotent 404s), 2/13 tickets DONE (REB-155, REB-352 back at distinct-only). Remaining ~54k across 11 tickets. Script: $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp/jira_comment_cleanup.py. Continuing.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

13 Jira issues are pinned at Jira's 5000-comment HARD cap, inflated by the pre-P1
non-idempotent re-posting bug (local truth 22-76 distinct comments each; Jira holds ~65
copies of each until it hit 5000). P1 (landed a6cdad0e2) STOPS new duplication, but the caps
remain — so NEW comments on these 13 tickets still silently fail to sync
(`COMMENTS_PER_ISSUE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED`) until the duplicates are purged.

13 issues (rebar-id -> Jira key, local-distinct / Jira-total): REB-155(5fd2, 76/5000),
REB-352(e0e2, 64), REB-428(8937, 31), REB-341(db7b, 30), REB-289(b744, 29), REB-427(822a, 27),
REB-183(7284, 25), REB-327(d251, 25), REB-424(2359, 25), REB-425(33d0, 25), REB-190(75a9, 22),
REB-426(59ef, 22), REB-453(b4d4, 22).

## Concrete mechanisms (resolve the plan-review blocks — code-grounded, reused not reinvented)

**Bot-authored identification (safety-critical filter).** Jira REST returns each comment `body`
as an Atlassian Document Format (ADF) dict, NOT a plain string, so the marker must be detected on
the RENDERED text, never by substring-searching the raw ADF object. A comment is
bridge-bot-authored IFF `adf.adf_to_text(body)` contains the reconciler marker
`RECONCILER_MARKER = "<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->"`
(`src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_comments.py:43`), appended to every outbound
comment by `_decorate_outbound_comment` (`:140-147`) as its own paragraph — which survives the
ADF round-trip and re-renders to the literal marker text (`outbound_comments.py:144-145`). Reuse
`adf.adf_to_text` (the same renderer `_normalize_comment_body` uses) to render before the marker
check; note `_normalize_comment_body` STRIPS the marker for the dedup key, so marker DETECTION
uses the pre-strip rendered text while the dedup GROUPING uses the marker-stripped normalized
text. This intrinsic marker is the PRIMARY and authoritative identifier — robust to accountId /
display-name changes, unlike an author field. A comment whose rendered text lacks the marker is
treated as human/foreign and is NEVER a deletion candidate.
Secondary cross-check: the backup captures every comment's `author.accountId`; the script
asserts that all marked (candidate) comments across the 13 tickets resolve to a single bot
`accountId` and records it — a divergent accountId on a marked comment aborts the run for
manual review (defense in depth; the marker still governs).

**Dedup key = the reconciler's OWN normalization (parity guarantee).** Group candidate comments
by `outbound_comments._normalize_comment_body(body)` — imported and reused verbatim, NOT
re-implemented — which does ADF->text via `adf.adf_to_text` then strips the marker and
leading/trailing whitespace (`outbound_comments.py:116-137`). Using the identical function the
reconciler uses to decide "already mirrored" guarantees the cleanup's notion of "distinct body"
matches the reconciler's, so post-cleanup the reconciler sees exactly one copy per distinct body
and emits zero re-adds. Machine-canary comments (`_EXCLUDED_COMMENT_PREFIXES`:
"BRIDGE_CANARY_ALERT:", "Still stale as of") carry volatile timestamps -> each is a distinct
normalized body -> each is kept (never a "duplicate" to purge) — correct and safe.

**Pagination (fetch ALL 5000, not the first page).** Reuse the live-validated
`acli.py::get_comments(key)` with `--paginate` + `_parse_paginated_comments` (the P1 fix,
confirmed returning 5000/5000 for REB-155) to build the full comment set for backup + delete-list.
Do NOT hand-roll a single-page fetch. NOTE: `_parse_paginated_comments` returns only a flat
`list[dict]` and DISCARDS the response envelope, so it does NOT surface Jira's reported `total`.
The completeness check therefore obtains the total via a SEPARATE, dedicated metadata call —
`GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/comment?maxResults=0` returns `{total, maxResults, startAt,
comments: []}`; the script reads `total` from that envelope (new script-level work, not provided
by the reuse target) and reconciles `len(fetched) == total` per ticket, REFUSING to proceed if
they differ (guards a silently-truncated fetch producing an incomplete/dangerous delete list).

**Keep-one rule.** For each distinct normalized body among MARKED comments on a ticket: keep the
OLDEST copy (min `created` timestamp) and delete the rest via
`DELETE /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/comment/{id}`. A distinct body with only ONE marked copy is
NEVER deleted. Unmarked (human) comments are never touched.

**Rate limiting + transient-failure handling.** Jira Cloud enforces API rate limits over
~64k+ DELETEs (≈4900 × 13). Throttle to a conservative default (≤10 req/s, i.e. ≥100ms
inter-call sleep). IMPORTANT: the existing `_direct_rest_delete` -> `_rest_urlopen_with_retry`
path does NOT retry HTTP 429/5xx — it re-raises 4xx/5xx immediately as deterministic
(`acli_rest.py:62-64`). SINGLE chosen route (no alternatives): wrap each DELETE call through
`dispatch_one._call_with_retry`, which is the repo's one existing 429+Retry-After-aware retry
(honors `Retry-After`, exponential backoff). Do NOT hand-roll a second retry layer. Deletions
are logged (ticket, comment id, normalized-body hash, outcome) to an append-only artifact
`artifacts/jira-cleanup/deletions-<ts>.jsonl` (one JSON object per line) as they happen, so a
mid-run rate-limit stall is resumable.

**Idempotent + resumable.** Re-running is safe: a comment already deleted (404 on DELETE) is
treated as success. On partial failure, re-run `--dry-run` to see the remaining duplicates and
resume `--execute`; the keep-one grouping is recomputed from the live paginated set each run.

**Rollback (irreversible-op mitigation).** Jira comment deletion cannot be undone via API. The
pre-deletion backup JSON (id, body, author, created, per ticket) is the restore artifact.
Concrete restore procedure: a `--restore <backup.json> --ticket <key>` script mode re-POSTs each
DELETED comment's ADF body (`POST /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/comment`, cross-referencing the
deletions-<ts>.jsonl log to restore only what THIS run deleted). Target state after restore: the
ticket again holds one instance of each restored distinct body (content-restore, NOT id-restore —
re-created comments get NEW ids/timestamps, and the reconciler marker is re-appended by the
normal outbound decorate path on the next sync). The backup MUST exist and pass its completeness
check before any DELETE fires. Because restore only re-creates content (not the exact 5000-copy
inflated state), rollback is a safety net for accidental over-deletion, not a bit-exact undo.

## What — irreversible; execute ONLY under this T4 safety plan (inlined)

- [ ] **Pre-deletion backup:** export FULL comment payload (id, body, author.accountId,
      author.displayName, created) for all 13 tickets to a versioned JSON artifact under
      `artifacts/jira-cleanup/backup-<ts>.json` BEFORE any deletion. Proving command: the script
      asserts, per ticket, `len(backup[key]) == jira_reported_total` and that the artifact is
      non-empty and parses; deletion is refused if the assertion fails.
- [ ] **Dry-run default:** the script defaults to `--dry-run`; it prints, per ticket, the count
      of marked comments, the count of distinct normalized bodies, the kept (oldest) id per body,
      and the delete-id count — and a machine-readable diff artifact. `--execute` is required to
      delete and runs ONLY after explicit human approval of the dry-run diff.
- [ ] **Keep-one dedup:** oldest-`created` kept per distinct normalized body; marker-authored
      only; single-copy bodies and all unmarked comments excluded (per mechanisms above).
- [ ] **Operator flow / rejection:** dry-run -> human review -> approve|reject. On reject or
      abort, NO deletion occurs and the run exits cleanly (backup + dry-run artifacts retained).
- [ ] **Sequencing:** run ONLY after P1 is live in the reconciler (CONFIRMED: reconcile-bridge
      run 29625135085 on commit a6cdad0e2 = green) — else the reconciler re-inflates within
      minutes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Backup artifact exists and passes its per-ticket completeness check — `len(fetched)` from
      the paginated `get_comments` equals `total` read from the dedicated
      `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/comment?maxResults=0` envelope — BEFORE any deletion. Proving
      command: `python -m json.tool artifacts/jira-cleanup/backup-<ts>.json >/dev/null` + the
      script's count-reconciliation log line per ticket.
- [ ] Dry-run diff (delete-id list + kept-oldest-id per distinct body, per ticket) is produced
      and human-approved before `--execute`. [operator-attested]
- [ ] Deletion filter provably respects: (a) marked-only (RECONCILER_MARKER present), (b)
      keep-oldest-per-distinct-body, (c) never single-copy, (d) never unmarked/human. A unit test
      over a synthetic comment set asserts the delete-list the planner computes matches (a)-(d);
      RED if the filter mislabels a human/unmarked or single-copy comment as deletable.
- [ ] Rate-limit safety: the DELETE loop routes through `dispatch_one._call_with_retry`
      (the single chosen 429/Retry-After route); a unit test asserts a 429 is retried (not
      dropped) and `Retry-After` is honored.
- [ ] Completeness-gate enforcement: a unit test asserts the script REFUSES to proceed (non-zero
      exit, no DELETE issued) when the paginated fetched-count != the `?maxResults=0` reported
      `total` for any ticket (guards a silently-truncated fetch).
- [ ] accountId cross-check: a unit test asserts (a) a marked comment whose `author.accountId`
      diverges from the single resolved bot accountId ABORTS the run for manual review (no DELETE),
      and (b) a homogeneous marked set records the bot accountId and proceeds.
- [ ] Approval-gate enforcement: a unit/integration test asserts `--dry-run` (default) issues
      ZERO DELETE calls, and that `--execute` is the ONLY path that issues DELETEs; on operator
      reject/abort no DELETE is issued and artifacts are retained.
- [ ] Reconciler quiescing during `--execute`: the run window is coordinated so the live
      reconciler does not write to the 13 tickets mid-deletion (either the pass cadence is paused,
      or the window is chosen between passes). [operator-attested]
- [ ] Post-execution, each of the 13 tickets is below the 5000 cap AND a reconcile pass emits 0
      comment re-adds for them (idempotent steady state). [operator-attested against a green
      reconcile-bridge run]
- [ ] The deletion-log artifact `artifacts/jira-cleanup/deletions-<ts>.jsonl` (one JSON object per
      line: ticket, comment id, normalized-body hash, outcome) exists for the executed run.
      Proving command: `wc -l artifacts/jira-cleanup/deletions-<ts>.jsonl` matches the reported
      delete count.
compiled_state.title: Jira data cleanup: purge duplicate comments on the 13 capped tickets (after P1 deploys; dry-run+approval first)
